purpose: Fauna registry. The l/v/m/s prefix system marks Tunyan-native variants of Earth-familiar species: muskrat = Earth-import, mluskrat = Tunyan native. Players who learn the prefixes can read native vs imported at a glance — a linguistic diagnostic same trick as the skin-color genetics.

prefix_system.comment: Tunyan native fauna carry a phonetic prefix distinguishing them from Earth-imports.

prefixes.s: shellfish + sea creatures (s'mollusca, slquirrel — yes, slquirrel breaks the prefix-domain rule and the linguists know)
prefixes.l: wild predators / pest-control variants (clougar = bobcat-analog, rlabbit = hare-analog, wleasels)

prefix_system.exceptions: frog, toucan (distinct from atoucan), elephant, hogs, wolves, sea turtle, capuchin monkey are Earth-import OR shared-name native species. Linguistic drift in some lineages.

fauna.kingdom_significance: Bahiij — elephant-revering culture. Mahouts are a respected profession class.

fauna.morphology: Massive — bigger than a killer orca. Razor teeth. Dense, powerful narwhal-style horn. Supercavitating speed (capable of crossing miles in minutes).
uses: ivory (the horn — most valuable single material on Tunya)
lore_significance: The single most dangerous animal on Tunya AND the single most valuable.

Sealies are the primary cause of the Fluxom sea-trade collapse. After Fluxom's ship dumped 200 refugees during a storm, sealies came for the bodies. They stayed for the ships. Fluxom condemned the kingdoms for sea-related actions to deflect from this; the kingdoms condemned Fluxom in return.
A successful sealie kill yields a vessel's-year of wealth in one night. Sealie hunters are the highest-paid profession on Tunya by far — and have a single-figure-percent voyage mortality.
Sealie ivory is so dense it doesn't float. Carved sealie-horn tools are passed down generations. The Sanguire warlord's greatsword has a sealie-ivory pommel.
narrative_hooks: Hunting a sealie is a tier-1 game event. Single-vessel encounter = likely vessel-loss. Multi-vessel coordinated hunt = the only viable approach.
Sealies appear to communicate through low-frequency sound. Whether they have intent toward the Fluxom refugee dump is open lore.

fauna.industry_significance: the s'mollusca's shell is the source of mollusca paper — Asbir's archive medium, the print press's substrate.
cephalopod ink is the Tunyan recording medium. Print press, registrars, authors all consume ink. The cephalopod-inker profession exists in coastal kingdoms.

uses: used_for_pest_control_by_humans (kept in granaries to hunt rodents)

fauna.lore: PEST. Mluskrats damage canal-bank infrastructure. Canal cleaners often double as mluskrat hunters.
Wleasels are deliberately maintained as anti-rodent predators in human grain storage.

fauna.linguistic_note: the prefix-domain rule places s- on shellfish + sea creatures, but slquirrel breaks the convention. The linguists know. The naming holds.

uses: forest_scavengers (clean the forest floor of decay)
fauna.lore: Mlonkeys are protected by Bahiij as part of the forest-cleaning pest-management culture.
Atoucans are deliberately attracted to orchards as living insecticide.

pest_management_culture.principle: Tunyans use predators rather than poisons.
pest_management_culture.cultural_significance: The Tunyan reluctance to use poison is partly practical (poison contaminates the dye-fixative urine ecology) and partly philosophical (the Field's preference for 'managed coexistence over elimination' propagates into mixed-Tunyan cultural attitudes).
